column1: flow
accesso all'app -> 1° accesso — type=association; label="start"; explanation="initial access"
1° accesso -> utente gia' registrato? — type=decision; label="start"; explanation="first access decision"
utente gia' registrato? -> registrazione — type=association; label="no"; explanation="no registration"
utente gia' registrato? -> login — type=association; label="yes"; explanation="already registered"
login -> home — type=association; label="login"; explanation="login to home"
registrazione -> home — type=association; label="registration"; explanation="registration to home"
flow 1: accesso all'app -> 1° accesso -> utente gia' registrato? -> (no) -> registrazione -> home
flow 2: accesso all'app -> 1° accesso -> utente gia' registrato? -> (yes) -> login -> home
this sequence diagram models the flow of a user accessing an application, with a decision point for registration status.
the user can either register or login if already registered.
the main flows show the two possible paths: registration or login.
the decision point is based on the user's registration status.
tragitto in corso -> avvia tragitto — type=transition
avvia tragitto -> tragitto terminato — type=transition
tragitto terminato -> calcolo emissioni — type=transition
tragitto in corso -> vuoi cambiare sezione? — type=decision
vuoi cambiare sezione? -> menu orrizontale — type=transition
flow 1: avvia tragitto -> tragitto terminato -> calcolo emissioni
this sequence diagram models the flow of a course with a start, end, and calculation of emissions.
the user can choose to change sections or not, and the system will either end the course or calculate emissions.
the diagram does not show any loops or repeated behavior.
visualizza report settimanale -> menu orrizontale — type=transition; label=si; explanation=if the user chooses to view the report, they are taken to the horizontal menu
menu orrizontale -> visualizza report settimanale — type=transition; label=si; explanation=if the user selects an option from the menu, they are taken back to the report visualization
flow 1: the user views the report, then navigates to the menu, and finally returns to the report
flow 2: none
the diagram models the interaction between the user and the system for viewing reports and navigating the menu
the user can view the report and then navigate to the menu, or they can choose not to view the report and exit the system
the system provides a horizontal menu for the user to navigate and view reports
visualizza dati personali -> inserisci nuovi dati — type=association; label="si"; explanation="insert new data"
inserisci nuovi dati -> menu orrizontale — type=association; label="si"; explanation="menu"
menu orrizontale -> visualizza dati personali — type=association; label="no"; explanation="no"
flow 1: visualizza dati personali -> inserisci nuovi dati -> menu orrizontale -> visualizza dati personali
the diagram models a user's interaction with a profile, allowing them to view their personal data, insert new data, and access a menu.
the user can either view their personal data or insert new data, which leads to a menu.
if the user chooses to insert new data, they are presented with a menu.
the user can then return to viewing their personal data.
the system does not allow the user to view their personal data after inserting new data.
utente -> applicazione — type=association; label="dispatch"; explanation="the user dispatches their credentials to the application"
applicazione -> server — type=association; label="insert credentials"; explanation="the application inserts the user's credentials into the server"
server -> database — type=association; label="verify credentials"; explanation="the server verifies the user's credentials against the database"
database -> server — type=association; label="data trovati"; explanation="the database returns the verified data to the server"
server -> applicazione — type=association; label="confronto con quelli inseriti"; explanation="the server confronts the verified data with the inserted data"
applicazione -> utente — type=association; label="login avvenuto"; explanation="the application logs in the user"
flow 1: dispatch -> inserire credenziali -> invio credenziali -> confronto con quelli inseriti -> login avvenuto
the diagram models a sequence of events where the user dispatches their credentials, which are then inserted into the application, verified by the server, and finally logged in.
the user's credentials are verified against the database, and if they match, the user is logged in.
the system uses a sequence of associations to connect the user, application, server, and database.
utente -> applicazione — type=association; label="dispatch"; explanation="utente dispatches to applicazione"
applicazione -> server — type=association; label="avvio percorso"; explanation="applicazione starts a new path to server"
server -> database — type=association; label="aggiunta percorso"; explanation="server adds a new path to database"
database -> server — type=association; label="nuovo percorso"; explanation="database receives a new path from server"

flow 1: utente -> applicazione -> server -> database -> utente - utente dispatches to applicazione, which starts a new path to server, which adds a new path to database, which receives a new path from server, and returns to utente

the diagram models a sequence of events where utente dispatches to applicazione, which starts a new path to server, which adds a new path to database, which receives a new path from server, and returns to utente.
the main flows are sequential and do not involve any decisions or loops.
the diagram does not show any errors or exceptions.
